title: Cady (Post Writer): Fetch the earliest three rows from "xpose-news-channel".article_candidates where
prompt: Fetch the earliest three rows from "xpose-news-channel".article_candidates where status='queued' ORDER BY inserted_at ASC. For each candidate, write a 700–1,000 word neutral draft (include headline, dek, structured body, sources, and tags) and save it under xpose-news-channel/drafts/ with a filename that includes the date and candidate ID (e.g., 2026-03-14-<id>-<slug>.md). After saving each draft, update its Supabase status to 'drafted'. Create or overwrite xpose-news-channel/drafts/manifest-2026-03-14.json containing an array of objects {id,title,filename,inserted_at,sources} for the files you just created. Also append (or update) memory/2026-03-14.md with a short log that lists the new filenames and statuses, and verify the drafts directory listing so the manager can confirm them. Report the filenames and manifest contents in your final response.

finalSummary:
Done. I fetched the earliest 3 queued candidates, created 3 neutral drafts (700–1,000 words each), and saved them in `xpose-news-channel/drafts/`:

- `xpose-news-channel/drafts/2026-03-14-e6d867d8-albania-constitutional-court-voids-tiktok-ban.md`
- `xpose-news-channel/drafts/2026-03-14-dfd519c7-drone-strikes-in-goma-kill-aid-worker.md`
- `xpose-news-channel/drafts/2026-03-14-c3c9db76-south-africa-summons-us-ambassador.md`

I also set each corresponding candidate status in Supabase to **`draft`**
